pattern: Only characters can be prepended, so the answer's reversed tail must reuse the longest palindromic prefix of the input; finding that prefix with a KMP failure function on s + separator + reverse(s) lets the rest be mirrored in front in linear time.
hints: You may only add characters at the front, so the original string stays as a suffix; minimizing additions means reusing the longest prefix of s that is already a palindrome.
Whatever prefix is not part of that palindromic prefix must be mirrored and placed before s, in reverse order.
Build the string s + '#' + reverse(s) and compute its KMP prefix-function; the last value is the length of the longest palindromic prefix, so prepend the reverse of the remaining suffix of s to s.

edge_inputs.description: palindromic prefix reused, only one char prepended
no palindromic prefix beyond the first char
empty string is already a palindrome
already a palindrome needs no additions
repeated char run is wholly palindromic
two equal chars then a tail

stress.description: moderate random lowercase strings over a small alphabet to surface palindromic prefixes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, max(0, size))
    alpha = 'abc'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
stress.note: small alphabet makes palindromic prefixes occur often; the quadratic oracle stays within a few thousand chars
